Second Report And Account Of Successor Trustee And Petition For Its Settlement And Approval; For Payment Of Trustee And Attorneys Fees And Reimbursement Of Costs Advanced And Reduction Of Trustees Bond By $31,000 From $283,000 To $252,00
Off Calendar Dec-02-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sheila Robello for Thomas Lucas (Trustee).

Ms. Robello addressed the issue raised in the Examiner's notes with regard to the multiple credit cards held by the Beneficiary stating that all of the credit cards are in the name and social security number of the Beneficiary, that they all have low balances, that they are considered loans to the Beneficiary and that they will not affect his Social Security benefits. Ms. Robello also stated that the expenditures are less than $535.00 a month, that the Trustee reviews the credit card bills and pays those that appear to be appropriate, that the Trustee has refused to pay some of the charges, that the Beneficiary pays the balance and that the Beneficiary is very independent. The Court expressed concern about the funds lasting the Beneficiary's lifetime, suggested consolidating the nine credit cards into less and that the Trustee closely monitor the expenditures. In addition, Ms. Robello stated that she is not aware of the $2,907.00 that was paid to Lillian Ng for representation of Xin Y. Wang. The Second Report and Account of Successor Trustee, etc. was taken under submission.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

Petition For An Order Appointing Thomas A. Lucas As Successor Trustee Upon Vacancy In The Office Of Trustee, And Setting Trustee'S Bond
Off Calendar Sep-22-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Schultz for Verna Shahenn (Current Trustee). Sheila Robello for Thomas Lucas. Ronald Wright in Propria Persona.

Mr. Schultz stated that the Current Trustee was willing to resign and that Thomas Lucas was willing to serve. Ms. Robello stated that Mr. Wright wanted Mr. Tomioka to be relieved as Guardian Ad Litem and there was no objection by Thomas Lucas. The Court granted said request. The final account of the Former Trustee must be filed by December 1, 2009. The Court granted the petition, (signing the order for the same in open court). The Current Trustee's account must be filed by December 1, 2010.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR #2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Appointment Of A Successor Trustee And Status On The Pursuit Of The Legal Action
Off Calendar Aug-26-2008 Continued To Sep-30-08 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Verna Shaheen. Grant Tomioka for Ronald Wright.

The Parties stated that they were working on a distribution plan. The matter was continued to September 30, 2008 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Leticia Perez CSR#7782. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

PETITION RE SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT TRUST
Off Calendar Jul-22-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Grant Tomioka for and with Ronald Wright. Lawrence Siracusa for and with Verna Shaheen (Trustee).

Mr. Tomioka stated that Mr. Wright wanted representation in several matters and that he wanted clarification as what Mr. Tomioka's duties were. Mr. Tomioka made it clear that he did not do trust litigation or probate litigation. Mr. Wright stated that he wanted Mr. Siracusa and Ms. Shaheen to pay for legal fees. Mr. Siracusa stated that Mr. Wright was not under a conservatorship and therefore could hire Mr. Tomioka and have the Special Needs Trust pay for representation. In addition, Mr. Siracusa stated that all accountings on the probate matter could be provided, that Ms. Shaheen was willing and ready to resign but that Mr. Wright would lose his public benefits if he served as trustee. The Court informed Mr. Wright that he could not be appointed as trustee and directed Mr. Wright to choose a Successor Trustee. Mr. Tomioka will prepare an order after hearing regarding denial of Mr. Wright's request to be appointed, the recreational benefits and continued representation. The matter was set for August 26, 2008 at 10:30 a.m. for status on the appointment of a Successor Trustee and for status on the pursuit of the legal action. The accounting was approved. The request for fees was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Karen Farnsworth CSR#5162. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy B. Mann.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Verna Shaheen (Trustee). Ronald Wright in Pro Per. Jennifer Smith from the Adult Protective Services.

Mr. Wright stated that he was willing to accept the Court's recommendation in appointing the attorney for Guardian Ad Litem. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Giselle Casey CSR#8098. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Neu.

PETITION RE SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT TRUST
Off Calendar Apr-02-2008 Continued To Jul-22-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. LARRY SIRACUSA FOR THE PETITIONER VERNA SHAHEEN;
RONALD WRIGHT, APPEARING IN PRO PER.

MR. WRIGHT REQUESTED FOR A COURT APPOINTED ATTORNEY AND IS ASKING A CONTINUANCE FOR 90 DAYS.
MR. SIRACUSA REPORTED TO THE COURT THAT NO ATTORNEY CAN BE APPOINTED FOR MR. WRIGHT SINCE THIS IS NOT ON A CONSERVATORSHIP PROCEEDING. HE SUGGESTED TO BRING THE MATTER ON AN EX-PARTE PETITION FOR THE APPOINTMENT OF A GUARDIAN AD LITEM NOT LISTING ANY ATTORNEY'S NAME. THE COURT CONTINUED THE MATTER TO JULY 22, 2008. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Leticia Perez, CSR# 7782. Clerk: Cynthia Herbert/ S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: J. Garrido.
